title: Prescription Expense Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Prescription Expense Assistance 50 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions AIDS/HIV Cancer Medicare Beneficiaries Older Adults Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, disease management education, referrals to resources for basic needs, medical and dental services, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BORLAND FREE CLINIC PRIMARY CARE COMMUNITY CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Providies a community clinic offering primary and some speciality care. Also offers gynecological services, mental health care, physical therapy, diabetes education classes, prescription medicine discounts, assistance in applying for OHP, referrals to outside providers, and information about other social services. 3550 SW Borland Road Tualatin, OR 97062 Eligibility: Individuals who are at or below 400% of the Federal Poverty Line (FPL) Hours: Monday/Thursday 3pm-7pm, Wednesday 9am-1pm Email (503) 974-8887 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CANCERCARE CO-PAYMENT ASSISTANCE FOUNDATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides co-payment assistance for prescribed chemotherapy or targeted treatment drugs to eligible applicants. 485 Madison Avenue New York, NY 10022 Eligibility: To qualify for assistance, client must meet certain financial, medical and insurance criteria. Hours: Monday-Thursday 6am-4pm, Friday 6am-2pm Email (866) 552-6729 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAREASSIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance paying for insurance premiums, prescriptions, and medical service co-pays. Assists with 100% payments for a limited number of medical services and medications necessary for HIV treatment for uninsured persons. 800 NE Oregon Street #1105 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: HIV positive Oregonians with income up to 550% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment only Email (971) 673-0144 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY AND CENTRAL COAST ST JOSEPH FAMILY SHELTER MISSION BENEDICT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. When funding is available, offers emergency financial assistance for electricity, gas, water/sewer, and prescription costs. 840 S Main Street Mount Angel, OR 97362 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 3pm-6pm, Wednesday 11am-2pm, Thursday-Friday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 385-3231 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE HELPING HAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency financial assistance to address basic needs, including emergency lodging for up to three nights, prescription assistance for non-narcotic medications (twice per 12-month period), auto repairs up to $200 (once per 12-month period), laundry vouchers up to $10 per individual and $20 per family (twice per 12-month period), and rent payment or deposit assistance up to $300, but not more than 70% of one month's rent (once per 18-month period). 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: -Laundry vouchers: Unrestricted -Prescription assistance: Must have a written or phoned-in prescription from Walmart or Safeway -Rent assistance: Must be facing immediate eviction, potential homelessness, or experiencing homelessness -Lodging: Must be transitioning to permanent housing -Auto repairs: Must be in need of transportation to employment, medical appointment, or school Hours: Tuesday-Friday 9am-11:30am by appointment only (541) 276-3418 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with utility bills, one-time rent payments, and prescriptions. May have personal items such as clothes, hygiene products, diapers, and pet supplies, depending on donations received. 2310 1st Street Suite 2 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 842-5261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONGREGATIONS HELPING PEOPLE PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers financial assistance up to $50 to help cover the cost of prescriptions. Only able to assist with narcotic pain medications if accompanied by antibiotics. Prescriptions must be filled at the Safeway Pharmacy (12th and Center Street). All funding is first-come, first-served. 1977 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97303 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 391-4365 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8:00am-5pm Email (541) 322-7155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 370-2810 401 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING LA GRANDE HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 2102 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 612-4028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING ONTARIO HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 1021 SW 5th Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING PENDLETON HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 322 SW 3rd Avenue Pendleton, OR 978012174 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 104 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING THE DALLES HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 400 E Scenic Drive The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 276-1037 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF EUGENE FISH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prepared food boxes, prescription assistance, propane assistance, and hygiene items. Prescription payments are made directly to the dispensing pharmacy. Households can receive a food box and hygiene items once per month and fill a 5-gallon tank once every two months. Prescriptions are one-time for most medications, up to $100. 440 Maxwell Road Eugene, OR 97404 Eligibility: Food boxes, hygiene items and propane: Unrestricted Prescription assistance: Must have a prescription from a doctor Hours: Food: Tuesday/Thursday 10:30am-1:30pm to arrange food box; Pickup food box 2pm-3pm on requested day Propane and prescription assistance: Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday 10:30am-1:30pm Closed holidays (541) 689-9750 Get Directions More Details FISH OF LEBANON BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, utility assistance, heating fuel assistance, prescription assistance, and clothing. Also offers financial assistance to obtain a valid ID card or driver's license through the Oregon Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV). Assistance is available once per year for each service. 145 W Ash Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 259-3200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOOD RIVER COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides HIV testing, expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 1109 June Street Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 386-1115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUNCTION CITY LOCAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and clothing closet that includes shoes. Provides financial assistance for electric bills, prescriptions, and propane gas. Also provides bus tokens. Delivery is available for people who cannot come to the food pantry due to medical or transportation issues or other extenuating circumstances. 210 E 6th Avenue Junction City, OR 97448 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday 9am-1pm, Thursday 3pm-7pm Email (541) 998-3992 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KAISER PERMANENTE HEALTH CARE KAISER PERMANENTE NORTHEAST REGION MEDICAL GROUP MEDICAL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers temporary financial assistance and charity care to low-income, uninsured, and under-served patients of Kaiser Permanente providers. Also provides financial counseling for outstanding Kaiser Permanente medical and pharmacy bills if patient is unemployed or without other significant income. 500 NE Multnomah Boulevard Suite 100 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Patients of Kaiser Permanente Medical Group who have an outstanding balance with Kaiser Permanente and an annual gross household income at or below 400% of the Federal Poverty Level. Must have exhausted all other financial assistance and insurance options. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 813-2000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES COTTAGE GROVE OFFICE SENIOR CONNECTIONS Print & Share X older with physical disabilities Hours: By appointment only (541) 682-7806 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEUKEMIA AND LYMPHOMA SOCIETY FINANCIAL SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides medical expense assistance programs to help with insurance co-payments and/or insurance premium costs for prescription drugs related to blood cancer treatment. Also, may Provide financial assistance for ground transportation, air travel, and lodging-related expenses. 3 International Drive Suite 200 Rye Brook, NY 10573 Eligibility: -Must have a confirmed blood cancer diagnosis -Must have income no higher than 500% of the Federal Poverty Level -Must be a United States citizen or permanent resident Additionally, for the Co-Pay Assistance Program: Must have medical and/or prescription insurance Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-6pm Email (877) 557-2672 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH ALBANY HIV/AIDS RAPID TESTING AND PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential rapid testing for HIV/AIDS, syphilis, and hepatitis C. Offers prevention education and treatment support. Assists with access to HIV medications, transportation to medical appointments, transitional housing, disease management education, and referrals for basic needs, employment, and substance use treatment. 1050 SW 7th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 967-3888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT EMERGENCY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers limited financial assistance with rent, electric, gas, water bills, and life-sustaining prescription medications. The amount of assistance varies depending on the amount due and if assistance has been received previously. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS MEDNET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prescription assistance to individuals once per calendar year up to $60. 1855 Thomas Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Must be at or below federal poverty level and have no insurance Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 435-7080 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION CURRY MEDNET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prescription assistance to individuals once per calendar year up to $60. 937 Chetco Avenue Suite D Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Must be at or below federal poverty level and have no insurance Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 469-3155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION OREGON PRESCRIPTION DRUG PROGRAM ARRAYRX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps Oregonians purchase prescription drugs at up to an 80% discount on generic drugs, and up to 20% off brand-name drugs. Website maintains list of participating pharmacies and current drug prices. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (800) 913-4284 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATIENT ADVOCATE FOUNDATION CO-PAY RELIEF DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides direct payment for co-pays, co-insurance, and deductibles for patients who need financial assistance. 421 Butler Farm Road Hampton, VA 23666 Eligibility: Insured individuals with prescription coverage and income below 500% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Hours: Monday-Friday 5:30am-2pm Email (866) 512-3861 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS MONMOUTH RESOURCE CENTER RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource drop-in center with the following services: comprehensive information and referral, food boxes, a proof of homelessness document for a free Oregon identification card, prescription expense assistance, hygiene items, diapers, formula, transportation passes, and laundry passes. Assistance is based on available funding and is not guaranteed. 1407 Monmouth Independence Highway Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Unrestricted Transportation passes: Once per month for medical appointments, job interviews, or other appointments supported by documentation Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 751-1644 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY JACKSON COUNTY SALVATION ARMY SOCIAL SERVICES ASSISTANCE FOR OLDER ADULTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with rent, utilities, water, and prescriptions as funding is available. May also assist with transportation needs. 922 N Central Avenue Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 65 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm (541) 773-6965 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY PORTLAND TABERNACLE SENIOR PRESCRIPTION DISCOUNT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prescription assistance to older adults. Must change prescription to Salvation Army's authorized pharmacy. 8495 SE Monterey Avenue Happy Valley, OR 97086 Eligibility: Age 60 and older who meet income guidelines and are either uninsured or underinsured Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm, by appointment only Email (503) 239-1224 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SILVERTON AREA COMMUNITY AID TRANSPORTATION AND PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance for transportation costs including gasoline vouchers and bus tickets. Also assists with prescription medications (with some exceptions). Gas vouchers must be used at specified location and prescriptions must be filled at one of two participating local pharmacies. 1030 N 1st Street Suite A Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Households must meet Silverton Area Community Aid Income Guidelines Hours: Monday 11am-2pm, Tuesday 3pm-7pm, Thursday 9am-noon Call to schedule an appointment outside of walk-in hours Email (503) 873-3446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER COMMUNITY ASSISTANCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services including assistance with mortgage, electric, water and heating bills, prescriptions, shower and laundry vouchers, and transportation passes. Provides sleeping bags, tents, tarps, hats, gloves, and hand warmers during the winter season. All services are dependent on available funds. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-3:30pm, Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 997-2816 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOCIETY OF ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIAL SERVICES OF BEND PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides up to $100 on eligible month for prescription assistance; no assistance for pain medication. 950 SE 3rd Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: -Individuals unable to pay for the cost of much needed prescription medication -Once every other month for up to 6 months Hours: Monday 2pm-6pm, Tuesday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 389-6643 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH LINCOLN RESOURCES VOUCHER ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric, heating fuel, and water bills. Also assists with rent, gas money, and prescriptions. 3710 Crestline Drive Waldport, OR 97394 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Individuals requesting assistance must be full-time residents. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 563-3710 4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST LUKE CATHOLIC CHURCH FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance with medical care, prescriptions, past due electric, natural gas, and water bills. Does not provide assistance with gasoline, rent, or propane. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and is not guaranteed. This resource may only be accessed once in a 12 month period. 417 Harrison Street Woodburn, OR 97071 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only (971) 338-1074 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes, clothing for adults, household items (kitchenware and small appliances), prescription assistance, gas money (for job interviews or appointments), emergency motel vouchers, and ID assistance. 51484 Morson Road La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-noon and 12:30pm-2pm Email (541) 536-6135 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL MYRTLE CREEK FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency aid to help pay electricity, natural gas, heating fuel and prescriptions when funding is available. Utility assistance is limited to the last $50 of the bill and cannot provide deposits for service connection. Prescription assistance depends on the type of medication. 116 N Main Street Myrtle Creek, OR 97457 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday/Wednesday 9am-1pm (541) 863-5489 Get Directions More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY LINDHOLM SOCIAL SERVICE OFFICE EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, household items, small appliances, furniture, beds, propane vouchers for heating, emergency prescription assistance, and bus tokens when available. Assistance depends on the zip code. 456 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm by appointment only Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL ROSEBURG PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prescription expense assistance typically limited to $25 once per year. This program is funded through donations. 630 W Stanton Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Once per year Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm Email (541) 580-0680 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIETY SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift shop, household goods, bus passes for doctor appointments (no Greyhound tickets), prescription assistance, utility assistance (electric, housing gas, water, and propane), rent payment assistance, sheltering supplies (sleeping bags, tarps, and tents) and laundry assistance. Assistance is on a first-come, first-served basis and subject to available funds and amount of services previously given to an individual. Donations accepted. 132 SE H Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 10am-2pm, closed major holidays Email (541) 479-0926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SWEET HOME EMERGENCY MINISTRIES FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides up to $100 of financial assistance for past due water, electricity, and rent bills. Also provides assistance with prescription medications excluding those that are pain medications or mood-altering, gasoline vouchers for verified out of town doctor's appointments only, and propane vouchers up to $25 that can be used at the Sweet Home RV Center. Individuals may only receive assistance once during a 12 month period. 1115 Long Street Sweet Home, OR 97386 Eligibility: Households with income at or below 185% of the FPL. Those seeking assistance with electricity, natural gas or water must have a shut-off notice. Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday 9am-11:30am, closed 2nd Saturday of the month Email (541) 367-6504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER TURNING POINT COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER/FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Hosts a food pantry that also provides rental assistance for eviction prevention when funding is available, utility assistance, rental deposit assistance, prescription expense assistance, clothing, gas vouchers for medical appointments, and a thrift store. Offers application assistance for OHP,SNAP,TANF, LIHEAP, and Community Action Team (CAT) Housing Solutions. Access to faxing, scanning, emailing, and printing services. 220 E Columbia River Highway Clatskanie, OR 97016 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines for food pantry program Hours: Client Services: Monday 10am-12pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm Food Pantry: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 11am-2:30pm Thrift Store: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm Email (503) 728-3126 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES SOCIAL SECURITY ADMINISTRATION EXTRA HELP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance to Medicare Prescription Drug program recipients who have limited resources with prescription costs, including monthly premiums, annual deductibles, and prescription co-payments. Eligibility: -1 person household income limit of $23,475 and resource limit of $17,600 -2 person household income limit of $31,725 and resource limit of $35,130 Resource limits do not count home, one car, burial plot, furniture/ other household and personal items, and up to $1,500 for burial expenses if money has been set aside. Hours: Monday-Friday 4am-4pm (800) 772-1213 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help with rent, utilities (electric, natural gas, oil, propane, wood and water), prescriptions, identification cards, and other urgent needs. Usually only able to pay a portion of the requested amount. Funding is limited and assistance is not guaranteed. Can provide referrals to other agencies for further needs. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 752-4688 Get Directions Visit
